free_text: Study of the Zr-90(D,P)Zr-91 Reaction.
Date is Corrected

According to O.Schwerer's Remarks.

.DWBA and Optical Model Analysis.
.The Rutgers-Bell Tandem Van De Graaff
Accelerator.
A Relative Error of 3% and an Absolute Error of 8% is
Assigned For the Cross-Section Measurements.
.Accuracy of the Scattering Angles.
A 2.4 Mg/Cm**2 Target of 99% Enriched Zr-90 was Used.
.0.1-Mm Li-Drifted Silicon Detectors were Used
In Coincidence with the 2-Mm Detectors.
A 6.2 Mg/Cm**2 Target of 90% Enriched Zr-91 was Used.
For the Proton Elastic Scattering Polarization
Measurements an 84-Mg/Cm**2 Carbon Foil was Used.
.The Uncertainty is Reported by Authors.
.2-Mm Li-Drifted Silicon Detectors were Used For
Proton Cross-Section Measurements.
.Two Counter Telescopes,Each Consisting of Three
Silicon Surface-Barrier Detectors,400,400 and 500 Mu in
Thickness,were Used For the Polarization Measurements.

.Data-Point Reader Uncertainty.
.Data-Point Reader Uncertainty.
- By Authors - In Order to Investigate the Possibility
Of Resonances in Investigated Energy Range, the
91-Zr(P,P)91-Zr Cross Sections at 130, 150 and 170
Degrees were Measured in 100-KeV Steps from 14.4 to
16.4 MeV. These Steps are Small Enough to Detect
Any Resonance Phenomena Which would Affect this
Experiment, Since the Targets Used were at Least 100
KeV Thick.
- By Compiler - These Data are Named As
90-Zr(D,P)91-Zr.  But Compiler Decides to Assume the
Text As the True.
